name: Vertex Tax API
what_it_does: Enterprise-grade tax calculation platform that computes sales tax, VAT, and GST for transactions across global jurisdictions, integrating with ERP and commerce systems via REST and SOAP APIs.
use_cases: Calculating accurate sales tax or VAT for a transaction at checkout time from an agent-driven commerce workflow
Determining product taxability and applicable tax rates by jurisdiction for catalog management agents
Retrieving tax determination results to include on invoices generated by billing automation agents
Integrating tax calculation into ERP order-processing pipelines that agents orchestrate
Auditing transaction tax records and generating tax liability reports for compliance workflows
not_for: Small businesses or startups — Vertex is priced for enterprise; use Anrok or TaxJar for SMB scale
Simple US-only sales tax with minimal SKU complexity — lighter tools handle this adequately
Self-serve onboarding requiring immediate API access without a sales engagement
best_when: You are a large enterprise with complex multi-jurisdiction tax requirements, existing SAP/Oracle ERP infrastructure, and need a battle-tested calculation engine with audit-ready records.
avoid_when: You are an early-stage SaaS company — the contract size, implementation complexity, and sales process are not appropriate for startups or small teams.

auth.notes: Vertex O Series uses OAuth2 client credentials for their cloud API. Legacy SOAP endpoints use basic auth or client certificates. API credentials provisioned through the Vertex customer portal after contract execution.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier; sandbox environment available after contract execution
pricing.paid_tiers: Enterprise contracts starting in the tens of thousands of dollars annually; pricing based on transaction volume and modules
pricing.notes: Vertex requires a sales engagement and signed contract before API access is provisioned. No self-serve signup. Pricing is not publicly listed and requires a quote.

performance.rate_limits: Enterprise SLA-based rate limits negotiated per contract; typically handles high-volume ERP transaction throughput

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Error responses include codes and messages but documentation on error conditions is spread across multiple guides. SOAP API errors are verbose XML fault structures. REST API errors are cleaner JSON.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Transaction IDs can be used to prevent duplicate tax postings; requires agents to generate and track unique transaction identifiers per request.
known_agent_gotchas: OAuth2 tokens have short expiry; agents must implement token refresh logic or face mid-session 401 failures on long-running workflows
Tax calculation results are jurisdiction-specific — agents must pass accurate ship-to and ship-from addresses or calculations will be wrong and hard to audit
The SOAP and REST APIs have different feature parity — some advanced tax scenarios are only available via the legacy SOAP endpoint

Vertex configuration (product taxability mappings, exemption certificates) is managed in a separate admin UI and does not update in real-time; agents may see stale rates briefly after configuration changes
No self-serve sandbox — agents cannot be tested without a provisioned account, making CI/CD integration difficult without a dedicated test environment from Vertex

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced on all endpoints. OAuth2 and optionally mTLS for enterprise deployments. SOC1 and SOC2 Type II certified. ISO 27001 certified. Enterprise-grade security posture expected given customer profile.
